data.name: Agent Skills
data.summary: Reusable agent skills, per-repo starters, and a self-hosted MCP runtime (Serena/Graphify/Omnigraph/Superpowers/Playwright).

data.rationale: Completing per-project isolation: after copying all 4 projects into their own graphs and verifying the reconcile (160 project nodes + 2 globals = 162 = memory; 163/163 edges), the 160 project nodes were deleted from `memory` (edges cascade on node delete). `memory` is now 2 nodes / 0 edges and matches cluster/seed/memory.jsonl. Pruning is gated by split-project-graph.py --prune-source, which refuses unless every node is mirrored, with the same type, in its project graph.
data.title: The `memory` graph holds ONLY global-scope Preferences

data.rationale: d0607bf declared 5 graphs + 5 relational edge types but nothing had applied them: the live server ran one `memory` graph on the old schema, so Tracks/Affects/Addresses/Implements/DependsOn were rejected and every seed line using them failed silently. Root cause: apply-cluster.sh sourced a ./.env that never existed (convention is .env.shared + .env.server) so it died at line 15 under set -e and had never run. Fixed the script (env files, docker network mcp-server_mcp-net, MSYS path conversion), converged the cluster, repaired 7 orphaned hub edges, and split all 4 projects into their own graphs with the new split-project-graph.py. Verified: 160 project nodes + 2 global Preferences = 162 = memory; 163/163 edges.
data.title: Per-project graph isolation deployed and back-filled (2026-07-16)

data.rationale: ADR 0003 (2026-07-16). The off-by-default mem0-fallback profile was never exercised, yet kept leaking back into docs and per-repo instructions as a live option (basic-analysis wired mem0 and no omnigraph at all while claiming 'Memory is Omnigraph now'). A fallback with a different data model is not a fallback: Mem0 stores blobs by user_id, Omnigraph stores typed nodes/edges, nothing migrates, so falling back meant losing the graph. It cost 4 containers (Postgres/pgvector, API, bridge, dashboard), a POSTGRES_PASSWORD, a DeepSeek key, and a second embedding model (bge-m3 1024-dim next to the graph's 768-dim nomic-embed-text — a standing dimension-mismatch trap). Its tooling had already rotted: the setup/start/stop/test/migrate scripts still health-checked mem0 on :8888 and their bare `docker compose` calls failed after the server/client compose split. Removed: servers/_fallback/, the compose profile + 4 services, the mem0/Postgres/DeepSeek env vars, the Mem0-era scripts, and the legacy docs/ARCHITECTURE.md + docs/TOKEN_SAVINGS.md. The stack now needs no Postgres and no LLM API key; Ollama stays optional. Insurance is backups (cluster/seed/*.jsonl, .graph-backup/ exports, MinIO), not a parallel product.
data.title: Remove the Mem0 fallback entirely — Omnigraph has no fallback

data.rationale: OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH_ID pins a bridge to exactly one graph and no tool (query/mutate/load) accepts a graph argument, so under per-project isolation an agent physically cannot reach the shared `memory` graph its skill tells it to recall globals from. Resolution: declare two servers per repo — `omnigraph` (OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH_ID=<repo>, read+write) and `omnigraph-globals` (OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH_ID=memory, read-only). Recorded in structured-memory/SKILL.md and both starters.
data.title: Reading global Preferences requires a second omnigraph MCP bridge

data.location: infra/mcp-servers/cluster/ (cluster.yaml, memory.pg, *.policy.yaml, queries/, seed/) declared in git; live state in MinIO s3://omnigraph/cluster. Converged by scripts/apply-cluster.sh; per-project split by scripts/split-project-graph.py.
data.name: Omnigraph cluster (declared config + live store)

data.statement: Declared cluster config is NOT live until scripts/apply-cluster.sh runs against the target server. Before trusting cluster.yaml or memory.pg, verify against the live server: graphs_list must list the declared graphs and schema_get must show the declared edge types. A seed or mutation using an undeclared edge type fails silently.

A merge-loaded Decision is NOT semantically searchable, and on v0.8.1 you cannot fix it in place. `load --mode merge` does not compute the @embed("rationale") vector, and merging a Decision that HAD one ERASES it (the upsert replaces the row) — an unembedded Decision is dropped from nearest() entirely, not ranked low. Verified 2026-07-16: both re-embed paths fail with the same Lance bug on a populated graph (`overwrite` -> stage_create_btree_index; `merge` carrying hand-supplied vectors -> LanceError(Arrow) same-length), and `omnigraph embed --reembed_all` cannot target a local endpoint. So scripts/populate-embeddings.py only works against a fresh/empty graph. Treat this as a known limitation: the node stays findable by traversal and full-text; the only re-embed path is a rebuild via scripts/dedup-graph.py (which reloads into an empty store). Never claim semantic search covers a Decision you merged without confirming with a nearest() query.

Omnigraph is the only memory layer — there is no fallback. If it is down, work the session without recall (read the repo, CHANGELOG, ADRs) and persist when it returns; never stand up a side-channel store. Memory is an accelerator, not a correctness dependency. Recovery order: cluster/seed/*.jsonl (the boot seeder re-merges them), then a .graph-backup/ NDJSON export, then MinIO's bind-mounted store.

Keep cluster/seed/*.jsonl in sync with live before relying on them: the seed loader merges by @key(slug) on every boot, so a stale seed silently overwrites newer live values. Refresh with scripts/split-project-graph.py <project> --write-seed.

DO NOT enable omnigraph-sync.timer or dedup-graph.timer until both are multi-graph aware. omnigraph-sync.sh/sync-windows.ps1 sync exactly one graph (GRAPH defaults to `memory`) and step 5 pulls central into local with `load --mode overwrite` -- pointing a pruned local at a stale central RESTORES the pruned data and undoes the migration. dedup-graph.py hardcodes `--graph memory` in 3 places. Consequence today: the 4 project graphs are never synced to central and exist only on the client that created them.

data.location: infra/mcp-servers/setup/{omnigraph-sync.sh,sync-windows.ps1,omnigraph-sync.timer} pushes a device/<host> branch to central and pulls back; infra/mcp-servers/scripts/dedup-graph.py + dedup-graph.timer runs hourly on coding.vm.
data.name: Omnigraph sync + dedup automation

data.example: Record notable changes in CHANGELOG.md and non-obvious decisions as docs/decisions/NNNN-*.md; small revertible commits.
data.name: Backtracking via CHANGELOG + ADRs

data.rationale: Per-agent status, detach/reattach over SSH, socket API for agent-to-agent orchestration. antigravity-remote-ui kept for the distinct IDE-streaming case.
data.title: Herdr is the recommended agent multiplexer over raw tmux

data.statement: No personal absolute paths or secrets in tracked files; use ${AGENT_SKILLS_ROOT}/${CODE_ROOT}/${SERENA_HOME} and gitignored .env.

data.rationale: Typed, queryable, reviewable memory keeps rules/decisions integrated; Mem0 auto-extracts opaque vector blobs. Mem0 kept as off-by-default fallback profile.
data.title: Omnigraph replaces Mem0 as default memory

data.statement: A skill's SKILL.md is the single source of truth; starters and root instruction files are thin pointers, never copies of the workflow.

data.rationale: Consolidated loose dirs (Gen/prompt/repositories -> prompts/), moved mcp-servers + antigravity under infra/, untracked graphify-out, de-personalized paths.
data.title: Repo restructured into skills/ starters/ infra/
